desc:
Bug 1921814 - add keyboard focus for tab group labels r=dao,dwalker,desktop-theme-reviewers,tabbrowser-reviewers

This patch makes tab group labels "focusable" using the keyboard when the tab strip contains both tabs and tab groups.

Differential Revision: https://phabricator.services.mozilla.com/D226154

lines.l: *          Can be from a different window as well
*          Can defer loading of the tab contents
*          The new index of the tab

* @param tabs array of tabs to add

+    "tab group label should have the button role"
+    "tab group label aria-label should default to 'unnamed' if not set"
+    "tab group label aria-description should provide the name of the tab group plus more context"
+    "tab group label aria-expanded should default to true, since tab groups default to not collapsed"
+    "tab group label aria-label should equal the name of the tab group"
+    "tab group label aria-description should provide the name of the tab group plus more context"
+    "tab group label aria-expanded should be false when the tab group is collapsed"
+ * Presses Cmd + arrow key/Ctrl + arrow key in order to move the keyboard
+ * focused item in the tab strip left or right
+  }, "Waiting for element to get keyboard focus");
+  const tabGroup = gBrowser.addTabGroup([tab2, tab3], { insertBefore: tab2 });
+  // The user normally needs to hit Tab/Shift+Tab in order to cycle
+  // focused elements until the active tab is focused, but the number
+  // of focusable elements in the browser changes depending on the user's
+  // UI customizations. This code is forcing the focus to the active tab
+  // to avoid any dependencies on specific UI configuration.
+  info("Move focus to the active tab");
+    "keyboard focus should move left from tab to tab group label"
+    "keyboard focus should move left from tab group label to tab"
+    "keyboard focus should move right from tab to tab group label"
+    "keyboard focus 'up' should move left from tab group label to tab with LTR GUI"
+    "keyboard focus 'down' should move right from tab to tab group label with LTR GUI"
+    "Validate that the keyboard can control the expanded/collapsed state of the tab group"
+    "keyboard focus should remain on tab group label after collapse"
+    "active tab should automatically change to the next visible tab if the active tab is in a tab group that gets collapsed"
+  Assert.ok(tabGroup.collapsed, "Tab group should be collapsed once again");
+  info("Validate that keyboard focus skips over tabs in collapsed tab groups");
+    "keyboard focus should move right collapsed tab group label to the first tab to the right of the tab group"

+      // Handles some keyboard interactions when the active tab is in focus
-     * @param {Node} startTab         A <tab> element to start searching from.
-     * @param {Number} opts.direction 1 to search forward, -1 to search backward.
-     * @param {Boolean} opts.wrap     If true, wrap around if the search reaches
-     *                                the end (or beginning) of the tab strip.
-     *                                If true (which is the default), start
-     *                                searching from the  next tab after (or
-     *                                before) startTab.  If false, startTab may
-     *                                be returned if it passes the filter.
-     * @param {Boolean} opts.advance  If false, start searching with startTab.  If
-     *                                true, start searching with an adjacent tab.
-     * @param {Function} opts.filter  A function to select which tabs to return.
-     * @return {Node | null}     The next <tab> element or, if none exists, null.
+     *   A `<tab>` element to start searching from.
+     *   1 to search forward, -1 to search backward.
+     *   If true, wrap around if the search reaches the end (or beginning)
+     *   If true (which is the default), start searching from the next tab
+     *   after (or before) `startTab`. If false, `startTab` may be returned
+     *   A function to select which tabs to return.
+     *   The next `<tab>` element or, if none exists, null.
// Handle keyboard navigation for a hidden tab that can be selected, like the Firefox View tab,
// which has a random placement in this.allTabs.
